params.fun_fact_topic: 10 surprising facts about dinosaurs for kids — sizes, sounds, what they ate, how they walked
10 amazing facts about carnivorous dinosaurs — T-Rex bite force, Velociraptor pack hunting, Spinosaurus swimming
10 cool facts about weird dinosaurs — Therizinosaurus' giant claws, Microraptor's four wings, Pachycephalosaurus' dome head

params.species_name: Tyrannosaurus Rex — apex predator of the Late Cretaceous, 12m long, 40 km/h, bite force 12,800 lb
Triceratops — three-horned herbivore of the Late Cretaceous, frilled skull, lived in herds, 9m long
Stegosaurus — Jurassic plated herbivore, thagomizer tail spikes, double row of bony back plates, walnut-sized brain
Velociraptor — small Late Cretaceous pack hunter, feathered, sickle claw, intelligent, 2m long
Brachiosaurus — Jurassic long-necked herbivore, reached 12m tall to feed on treetops, weighed 50 tons
Spinosaurus — semi-aquatic Cretaceous carnivore, sail-back, longer than T-Rex, swam and hunted fish in rivers
Allosaurus — Late Jurassic apex predator before T-Rex, slashing teeth, hunted in groups, 8.5m long
Pteranodon — Late Cretaceous flying reptile (technically a pterosaur, not a dinosaur), 7m wingspan, fished from the sky
Ankylosaurus — Late Cretaceous armored herbivore, club-tailed, low and tank-like, fused bone armor across back
Parasaurolophus — Late Cretaceous duck-billed herbivore, hollow crest amplified honking calls, 10m long

params.animal_name: Tyrannosaurus Rex — skeletal anatomy cutaway: massive skull, tiny forelimbs, powerful legs, serrated 12-inch teeth, S-curved neck
Triceratops — anatomy cutaway: three horns, bony neck frill, parrot-like beak, dental battery for grinding plants, four sturdy legs
Stegosaurus — anatomy cutaway: 17 back plates (thermoregulation), thagomizer tail with 4 spikes, walnut brain, twin-rowed plates
Velociraptor — anatomy cutaway: sickle claw on second toe, feathered arms (not wings), long stiff tail, hollow bones
Brachiosaurus — anatomy cutaway: 9m vertical neck, column legs longer in front than rear, small head, peg-like teeth for stripping leaves

params.ecology_topic_info: Triassic Period ecosystem (252-201 million years ago) — early dinosaurs Coelophysis & Plateosaurus, fern forests, dry red-bed climates, first crocodile ancestors, Pangaea supercontinent
Jurassic Period ecosystem (201-145 million years ago) — golden age of dinosaurs, Brachiosaurus & Stegosaurus & Allosaurus, lush conifer forests, first birds (Archaeopteryx), Pangaea splitting

Cretaceous Period ecosystem (145-66 million years ago) — peak dinosaur diversity (T-Rex, Triceratops, Velociraptor, Spinosaurus), flowering plants emerge, mammals diversify, ends with the K-Pg asteroid impact

params.theme_name: popular dinosaurs vocabulary — Tyrannosaurus, Triceratops, Stegosaurus, Velociraptor, Brachiosaurus, Spinosaurus, Allosaurus, Pteranodon, Ankylosaurus, Parasaurolophus
paleontology vocabulary for kids — fossil, extinct, herbivore, carnivore, omnivore, predator, prey, Mesozoic, dig site, paleontologist

params.topic: Dinosaur to modern birds evolution timeline — from feathered theropods (Velociraptor, Microraptor) through Archaeopteryx to modern birds; 165 million years of evolution
Top 10 biggest dinosaurs ever — Argentinosaurus, Patagotitan, Dreadnoughtus, Spinosaurus, Sauroposeidon, Brachiosaurus, Diplodocus, Apatosaurus, Mamenchisaurus, Shantungosaurus — with length + weight
